Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 92 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 221 upvotes, 26 comments
  3. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 43 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. NMAP in the Movies 99 upvotes, 13 comments
  5. Can I run AI locally? 1364 upvotes, 323 comments
  6. Sunsetting Jazzband 39 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 17 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Python: The Optimization Ladder 189 upvotes, 58 comments
  9. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 150 upvotes, 45 comments
  10. Atari 2600 BASIC Programming (2015) 53 upvotes, 13 comments
  11. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1053 upvotes, 440 comments
  12. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 118 upvotes, 41 comments
  13. Digg.com Closing Due to Spam 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 128 upvotes, 54 comments
  15. Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation 52 upvotes, 22 comments
  16. Show HN: Learn Arabic with spaced repetition and comprehensible input 21 upvotes, 7 comments
  17. Everything you never wanted to know about visually-hidden 17 upvotes, 5 comments
  18. Generalizing Knuth's Pseudocode Architecture to Knowledge 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 184 upvotes, 125 comments
  20. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 199 upvotes, 138 comments
  21. Nominal Types in WebAssembly 29 upvotes, 14 comments
  22. An Ode to Bzip 43 upvotes, 24 comments
  23. What happens when US economic data becomes unreliable 209 upvotes, 158 comments
  24. Digg is gone again 353 upvotes, 369 comments
  25. The $2 per hour worker behind the OnlyFans boom 58 upvotes, 49 comments
  26. XML Is a Cheap DSL 196 upvotes, 199 comments
  27. Hostile Volume – A game about adjusting volume with intentionally bad UI 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. In Praise of Stupid Questions 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 361 upvotes, 603 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 15 March 2026. Source here.
